title: Event envelope
description: Shared envelope every event in the toolkit-server substrate wears. Closed shape — 11 top-level fields, no additionalProperties at this layer. Per-type payload validation happens against blueprints/events/<type>.json; the envelope schema validates everything else. The leading underscore in the filename sorts this first in the directory listing and signals 'not an event type'. See docs/EVENT_SUBSTRATE.md §2 for field-by-field rationale.

event_id.description: UUIDv7. Time-ordered (the first 48 bits are a Unix-ms timestamp), globally unique, FK-targetable. Sortable by event_id gives wall-clock event order. Required for the sibling chain's query_resolutions.write_event_ids JSON-array FK semantics — do not weaken to UUIDv4 without a chain-level decision.

ts.description: RFC 3339 timestamp with UTC zone, millisecond precision. Redundant with event_id's embedded timestamp; kept as its own column so range queries on (ts BETWEEN ... AND ...) don't require parsing UUIDs. Always UTC; local timezones never appear in the events table.

actor.description: Who emitted the event. Inferred at dispatch from the transport that delivered the call — never caller-supplied. See EVENT_SUBSTRATE.md §4 for the inference rules.

kind.description: Closed enum. agent = stdio MCP transport (Claude Code, qwen-via-mcp, etc.); human = portal HTTP write paths; system = CLI subcommands, cron jobs, in-transaction hooks (which actually inherit from the parent — see §4).

id.description: For agent: the connecting MCP client's name+version from the initialize handshake (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7'). For human: a portal-session identifier ('portal-anonymous-<uuid>' until auth lands). For system: 'cli-<subcommand>' or 'cron-<job-name>'.

type.description: Event type name. Must match a registered type with a payload schema at blueprints/events/<type>.json. PascalCase, past-tense (BugResolved, not ResolveBug). Closed set — emitting an unknown type is rejected at the dispatcher with InvalidInput before any DB write. See docs/EVENT_CATALOG.md for the registered set.

entity.description: The primary entity this event acts on. Secondary entities go in refs.related_entities, not here.

kind.description: Entity kind. Currently: bug, suggestion, task, chain, benchmark_run, benchmark_metric. Adding a new kind requires the same PR-and-schema discipline as adding a new event type.

slug.description: The natural-key identifier for the entity. Toolkit-server uses slugs (e.g. 'forge-bug-title-omitted') as primary IDs throughout — numeric IDs never appear in event envelopes.

project_id.description: Project this entity belongs to. Required for project-scoped kinds (bug, task, chain) — mirrors the project-scoping convention from blueprints/forge-schemas/. Cross-cutting events (benchmark runs spanning projects, system-wide events) carry null and document why in their type schema.

payload.description: Type-specific payload. Validated against blueprints/events/<type>.json at write time. May be {} for types that record a state change with no parameters beyond the entity reference (rare; most types carry at least one field).

rationale.description: Why this event, in the actor's words. Required (non-null, non-whitespace-only) for actor.kind == 'agent' on actions whose manifest declares requires_rationale = true. Enforced at the dispatch boundary, NOT in handler code — see EVENT_SUBSTRATE.md §5. Boilerplate detection is a read-side concern (sibling chain), not enforced here.

refs.description: Causal and cross-entity references. caused_by_event_id is the structural backbone of the causal graph; related_entities is the cross-cutting metadata.

caused_by_event_id.description: For compensating events (BugTriageReversed undoing a BugTriaged): points at the event being undone. For cascade-emitted events (an in-transaction hook firing from a parent action): points at the parent. null for root events that aren't caused by another.

related_entities.description: Cross-entity references that aren't the primary entity. Example: BugRoutedToTask has entity = bug, related_entities = [{kind: task, slug: ..., project_id: ...}].

span_id.description: Per-MCP-request span identifier (UUIDv4 — random; no need for time order). Shared by every event and structured-log line emitted while serving one tools/call request. The join key between the events table and the future structured-log store (T5). See EVENT_SUBSTRATE.md §6.

schema_version.description: Envelope version. Currently 1. Bumping requires a migration that backfills or maps. Per-type evolution is via type renaming (BugResolvedV2), not per-type version.
